pet.name: Biscuit
pet.special_needs:
Large senior dog with weight management needs. Needs moderate
  exercise, joint support, slower-paced walks, and feeding consistency.
pet.characteristics: Large senior dogs typically require moderate, low-impact exercise such as slow-paced walks to protect aging joints; consistent feeding schedules with portion control and joint-supportive nutrition help manage weight and mobility. Monitor for arthritis, dental health, and organ function through regular veterinary check-ups. Provide orthopedic bedding, easy access to water and food bowls at appropriate heights, and a calm environment. Mental enrichment through gentle play and scent work supports cognitive health. Maintain consistent daily routines and watch for signs of pain, stiffness, or reduced appetite.
pet.species_profile_summary: Large senior dogs typically live 8-12 years and benefit from weight management, joint support, consistent feeding, and low-impact exercise tailored to their age and mobility.

age_context.summary: Large senior dogs typically live 8-12 years and benefit from weight management, joint support, consistent feeding, and low-impact exercise tailored to their age and mobility.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide measured portion according to veterinary feeding plan. Ensure fresh water is accessible at appropriate height. Observe appetite and water intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require consistent feeding schedules with portion control for weight management. Monitoring hydration and appetite daily helps detect early health changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide measured portion according to veterinary feeding plan. Ensure fresh water is accessible. Record any changes in appetite or water intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent feeding twice daily supports weight management and provides routine structure. Evening check allows observation of daily patterns.

raw_recommendations.name: Slow-paced indoor walk or gentle movement
raw_recommendations.notes: Low-impact movement suitable for rainy weather. Can include slow hallway walks, gentle play, or stair navigation at Biscuit's pace. Stop if signs of discomfort appear.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Large senior dogs need moderate, low-impact exercise to protect aging joints. Indoor alternatives accommodate rainy weather while maintaining mobility and joint health.

raw_recommendations.name: Mobility and comfort observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe gait, stiffness upon rising, ease of movement, and any signs of pain or limping. Note any changes in posture or reluctance to move.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from close observation of mobility changes. Early detection of stiffness or pain allows timely veterinary consultation and supports joint health management.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle enrichment and mental stimulation
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer calm enrichment such as scent work, puzzle feeders, or gentle play. Avoid intense activity. Suitable for indoor rainy-day engagement.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Mental enrichment through gentle play and scent work supports cognitive health in senior pets. Calm activities are easier to tolerate than intense exercise.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening bathroom and comfort check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe bathroom habits, energy level, and overall comfort before bedtime. Note any changes in frequency or consistency.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Monitoring bathroom habits and evening energy helps detect appetite, hydration, or digestive changes. Consistency in routine supports senior pet wellbeing.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly orthopedic comfort assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Check orthopedic bedding condition and comfort. Ensure food and water bowls are at appropriate height. Verify easy access to resting areas. Note any new stiffness patterns.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Large senior dogs benefit from environmental support including orthopedic bedding and accessible feeding stations. Weekly checks ensure comfort aids remain effective.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly veterinary check-in preparation
raw_recommendations.notes: Compile observations from daily monitoring: appetite patterns, mobility changes, bathroom habits, energy levels, and any behavioral shifts. Prepare questions for veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular veterinary check-ups monitor for arthritis, dental health, and organ function. Organized observation records support informed veterinary discussions.
